---
title: "Student Performance Analysis"
description: "A Python project analysing the relationship between study time and academic performance."
author: "Serena Emedeke"
date: 2026-04-16
categories: [Python, Data Analysis]
image: /images/student-project.png
image-alt: "Line chart showing study hours versus student scores"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
execute:
  engine: jupyter
  echo: true
  eval: true
---

## Overview

This project analyses the relationship between study hours and student performance using Python. It demonstrates how data can be organised, visualised, and interpreted to identify trends.

## Objectives

- Organise data in a clear and structured format  
- Analyse the relationship between study time and performance  
- Visualise results using Python  
- Interpret findings and draw conclusions  

## Python Code

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.DataFrame({
    "StudyHours": [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
    "Score": [50, 55, 65, 72, 85]
})

df

fig = px.line(
    df,
    x="StudyHours",
    y="Score",
    markers=True,
    title="Study Hours vs Score"
)

fig
```
## Interpretation

The chart shows a clear positive relationship between study hours and student performance. As study time increases, scores also increase, indicating a strong upward trend.

This suggests that increased study time has a direct impact on academic performance in this dataset.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Key Insight

The analysis indicates a clear positive relationship between study hours and academic performance. This suggests that consistent study time can have a meaningful effect on academic outcomes.
:::

## Reflection

This project demonstrates how data analysis can be used to identify relationships between variables. 

Through this task, I developed my ability to create datasets, generate visualisations, and interpret trends. In future, this analysis could be extended by including more data points or additional variables such as revision methods or subject difficulty.

This project demonstrates my ability to apply Python, data analysis, and data visualisation techniques to identify and communicate meaningful insights.
